display.purpose: Splash-page headlines and one dominant idea per landing surface.

label.purpose: Buttons, nav labels, badges, and compact interface labels.

motion.name: state-ease
motion.purpose: Default ease-out for hover, focus, and small state transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: High-energy primary action for docs splash and guide entry points.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #ff1070; color: #ffffff; border: 0; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; font-family: Metropolis, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; transition: background-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #c00050; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #ffd0e0; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Same-hue secondary action for guide and reference navigation.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffe1ec; color: #501060; border: 1px solid #ffc0d8; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; font-family: Metropolis, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; transition: border-color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), color 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #c00050; color: #c00050; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #ff1070; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Docs Card
components.description: Compact Starlight-style feature card using tonal separation instead of decorative shadow.
components.html:
Streaming First

Use low-level request and message helpers for chunks, trailers, cancellation, and flow control.
components.css: .ds-docs-card { background: #241024; color: #ffe8f0; border: 1px solid #501060; border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 1rem; font-family: Metropolis, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-docs-card h3 { margin: 0 0 0.5rem; color: #ffffff; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-docs-card p { margin: 0; color: #ffc0d8; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Splash Lab Panel
components.description: Signature splash pattern: bold claim, real Gleam code, and a compact reading path.
components.html:
Typed HTTP without hiding Gun.

Connections, stream messages, response bodies, TLS options, and WebSocket frames stay explicit.

client.new(request.Get, "/")\n|> client.send(connection: conn)
components.css: .ds-lab-panel { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 0.9fr) minmax(16rem, 1.1fr); gap: 1.5rem; background: #100b1d; color: #ffe8f0; border: 1px solid #501060; border-radius: 1rem; padding: 1.5rem; font-family: Metropolis, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-lab-panel h2 { margin: 0 0 0.75rem; color: #ffffff; font-size: clamp(2rem, 7vw, 4.75rem); line-height: 0.98; letter-spacing: -0.03em; } .ds-lab-panel p { margin: 0; color: #ffc0d8; line-height: 1.65; } .ds-lab-panel pre { margin: 0; overflow: auto; background: #241024; color: #ffe8f0; border: 1px solid #501060; border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 1rem; line-height: 1.65; }

components.name: Nav Pill
components.description: Active-state navigation treatment that stays in the magenta/violet family.
components.css: .ds-nav-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; color: #501060; background: #ffe1ec; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.375rem 0.625rem; font-family: Metropolis, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav-pill:hover { color: #c00050; } .ds-nav-pill:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #ff1070; outline-offset: 3px; }

narrative.overview:
Gluegun's visual system should feel like technical documentation with live current running through it: precise enough for production developers, vivid enough to be remembered after a quick package evaluation. The existing Astro/Starlight site already commits to a magenta-and-purple identity with Metropolis as the single family; future work should deepen that identity rather than replacing it with generic developer-docs gray.

The system rejects toy-like candy branding that undercuts technical trust. Energy should come from saturated accent, confident contrast, direct copy, and strong examples, not novelty shapes or decorative gimmicks.
keyCharacteristics: Saturated magenta and violet are identity colors, not occasional decoration.
Single-family Metropolis typography keeps the voice direct, friendly, and technical.
Documentation density should stay scannable: short sections, explicit examples, and clear module mapping.
Framework defaults are acceptable only when they preserve the bold Gluegun palette.

rules.name: The Same-Hue Neutral Rule
rules.body: Neutral UI should remain in the purple/magenta family. Do not introduce default slate or blue-gray ramps unless a third-party component forces it and the values are overridden at the boundary.

rules.name: The No Gradient Text Rule
rules.body: Do not use gradient text, even for hero headings. The brand already has enough charge in solid magenta and violet.

rules.name: The One-Family Commitment Rule
rules.body: Keep Metropolis as the system voice unless a future redesign deliberately introduces a contrasting family. Do not add monospace as lazy shorthand for "developer."

rules.name: The Tonal-First Rule
rules.body: Use magenta/violet surface shifts for depth before adding drop shadows.

dos: Do preserve the magenta/purple identity from `website/src/styles/custom.css`.
Do use solid Charged Magenta or Command Magenta for emphasis instead of gradient text.
Do keep examples and API claims concrete; the brand is playful, but the docs should stay precise.
Do verify AA contrast whenever using Soft Plum, Light Pink, or Blush Panel for text.
Do provide reduced-motion fallbacks for any future animated landing or docs interactions.
donts: Don't make the site feel like toy-like candy branding that undercuts technical trust.
Don't use gradient text (`background-clip: text` with a gradient background).
Don't introduce generic slate/blue-gray developer-docs defaults where same-hue purple neutrals can do the job.
Don't use side-stripe borders, glassmorphism, or identical card grids as default decoration.
Don't use monospace as lazy shorthand for technical credibility.
